concepts.name: Accelerator Spectrum
concepts.description: The continuum of processing hardware ranging from highly programmable general-purpose CPUs to highly efficient, workload-specific custom ASICs.

concepts.name: Generality Tax
concepts.description: The energy and compute overhead incurred by CPUs to support flexible instruction sequences, which is unnecessary for uniform neural network workloads.

concepts.name: SIMT Execution Model
concepts.description: A parallel computing architecture where a single instruction is mapped across a massive grid of threads to hide memory latency.

concepts.name: Warp Divergence
concepts.description: The performance degradation that occurs when threads within the same execution group take different branching paths and must run sequentially.

concepts.name: Systolic Array
concepts.description: A fixed grid of compute units where data flows between neighboring cells in a pipelined, wave-like pattern to eliminate instruction overhead.

concepts.name: Custom ASIC
concepts.description: Specialized silicon chips designed to execute a specific computational workload with maximum energy efficiency at the cost of programmability.

concepts.name: Wafer-Scale Engine
concepts.description: A massive, continuous piece of silicon containing hundreds of thousands of cores connected by an ultra-fast on-chip fabric to minimize communication latency.

concepts.name: Chiplet Architecture
concepts.description: A packaging design that combines multiple smaller semiconductor dies using high-bandwidth interconnects to bypass reticle limits and improve manufacturing yield.

concepts.name: Reticle Limit
concepts.description: The maximum physical area of a single silicon die that photolithographic equipment can expose in a single manufacturing pass.

concepts.name: Silicon Lottery
concepts.description: The manufacturing variance in semiconductor fabrication that results in differing maximum clock frequencies and power efficiencies among identical chip designs.

concepts.name: Memory Wall
concepts.description: The growing performance bottleneck caused by processor logic speeds increasing at a much faster rate than data access speeds.

concepts.name: Memory Boundedness
concepts.description: A condition where workload performance is strictly bottlenecked by data transfer speeds from memory rather than the system's compute capacity.

concepts.name: High Bandwidth Memory
concepts.description: A memory architecture that vertically stacks DRAM dies connected by through-silicon vias to provide massive data bandwidth on the processor's package.

concepts.name: Through-Silicon Vias
concepts.description: Microscopic vertical copper connections etched directly through stacked memory dies to drastically shorten signal paths and increase bandwidth.

concepts.name: Silicon Interposer
concepts.description: A passive silicon substrate with microscopic etched wiring layers that enables thousands of parallel connections between the processor die and memory stacks.

concepts.name: Roofline Model
concepts.description: A visual and analytical framework that evaluates achievable hardware performance by mapping workloads against peak compute throughput and memory bandwidth ceilings.

concepts.name: Arithmetic Intensity
concepts.description: The ratio of floating-point operations performed to the total bytes of data accessed from memory for a given workload.

concepts.name: Tensor Cores
concepts.description: Specialized arithmetic units designed to perform complete matrix-multiply-accumulate operations on small data tiles in a single clock cycle.

concepts.name: Hardware Precision Scaling
concepts.description: A hardware mechanism that automatically monitors tensor magnitude distributions and dynamically toggles between numerical precisions to maximize throughput.

concepts.name: Kernel Fusion
concepts.description: A software optimization technique that combines multiple sequential operations into a single execution step to eliminate intermediate memory round-trips.

concepts.name: Model FLOPS Utilization
concepts.description: The ratio of observed mathematical throughput during model execution compared to the theoretical peak compute performance of the hardware.

concepts.name: Thermal Design Power
concepts.description: The maximum sustained heat dissipation generated by a processor that the physical infrastructure must be designed to continuously cool.

concepts.name: Dennard Scaling
concepts.description: A historical observation that as transistors shrink, their power density stays constant, the breakdown of which led to the modern power wall.

concepts.name: Dynamic Voltage Frequency Scaling
concepts.description: A power management technique that adjusts a processor's clock speed and voltage in real time to maximize performance while staying within thermal limits.

concepts.name: Mixed-Precision Training
concepts.description: A training technique that uses high-precision storage for stability while executing forward and backward passes in lower-precision formats to accelerate computation.

concepts.name: Model Quantization
concepts.description: The process of reducing the bit precision of model weights to drastically decrease memory bandwidth requirements and improve token generation latency during inference.

concepts.name: Continuous Batching
concepts.description: An inference serving technique that dynamically schedules new requests at the iteration level to fill idle compute slots within an ongoing decode batch.

concepts.name: AllToAll Communication
concepts.description: A dense network communication pattern required by Mixture-of-Experts models where every accelerator must simultaneously exchange distinct data with every other accelerator.
